title: STR Regulatory Update — July 14, 2026
subtitle: 10 short-term rental regulatory changes across the US

changes.headline: Baytown, TX approves new STR ordinance — $350 annual permit, Fire Marshal inspection
changes.summary: Baytown City Council approved a new short-term rental ordinance on July 9, 2026 requiring $350 annual permits with Fire Marshal inspections at renewal, a 24/7 local contact within 50 miles, a 7% hotel-motel occupancy tax, stays capped at 30 days, and occupancy limited to 10 people. Parking requirements are tied to bedroom count.

changes.headline: Chatham County, GA imposes 120-day moratorium on new STR applications
changes.summary: Chatham County commissioners voted on July 10, 2026 to impose a 120-day moratorium on new short-term vacation rental (STVR) applications. No new STVR licenses will be accepted through November 7, 2026 while the county rewrites its 2021 STR ordinance. Existing permits remain valid.

changes.headline: Gwinnett County, GA STR ordinance confirmed — effective April 14, 2027
changes.summary: New coverage confirms Gwinnett County commissioners approved a Short-Term Rental Ordinance effective April 14, 2027, following a nine-month task force process. Requirements include annual licenses for stays under 30 days, a designated local property manager, and mandatory inspections. Corporate ownership limits are under consideration.

changes.headline: Buena Vista, CO Board of Trustees reviews STR Committee recommendations amid moratorium
changes.summary: Buena Vista's Board of Trustees held a work session July 14, 2026 to review non-binding recommendations from its Short-Term Rental Committee. The town's moratorium on new STR licenses has been in effect since January 13, 2026 through September 2026 while the STR code is rewritten. Ordinance No. 9 (Sept 2025) currently caps out-of-county licenses at 119 and in-county non-resident licenses at 30, with R-1 and R-1 OT zones prohibited for out-of-county owners.

changes.headline: Nevada Supreme Court: Clark County cannot deny STR permits solely due to septic systems
changes.summary: The Nevada Supreme Court issued a ruling on July 10, 2026 barring Clark County from denying short-term rental applications solely because a property uses a septic system. The host-favorable decision expands STR permit eligibility in the county.

changes.headline: Hawaii County Bill 147: Windward Planning Commission set to take up STR bill July 16
changes.summary: The Windward Planning Commission scheduled consideration of Bill 147 for July 16, 2026 after the Leeward Commission deferred it following a four-hour hearing that drew mostly opposition testimony from current STR and B&B operators. The Windward Commission's outcome will determine whether the bill advances to the County Council.

changes.headline: Starkville, MS second public hearing July 21 on 2% hotel tax extension to STRs
changes.summary: Starkville is advancing a proposed ordinance to extend its existing 2% hotel tax to Airbnb and VRBO stays, enabled by 2025 Mississippi state law (MS SB 2805). A second public hearing is scheduled for July 21, 2026. A $15 annual license fee is also proposed for operators. The Building & Housing Code Section 17.6 currently explicitly exempts STRs from licensing.

changes.headline: Strasburg Township, PA supervisors advance draft restricting new STRs to commercial zone only
changes.summary: Strasburg Township supervisors voted 3-0 on July 6, 2026 to advance a draft zoning amendment that would restrict new short-term rentals to the Gateway North commercial zone only. STRs are currently permitted in agricultural and rural residential zones by special exception, with some owners circumventing on-site residency requirements. The amendment moves to planning commission review with a possible final vote at the August 3 meeting.

changes.headline: Bella Vista, AR considers raising STR permit cap from 600 to 687 units
changes.summary: Bella Vista City Council is expected to vote at its July 27, 2026 meeting on raising the short-term rental permit ceiling from 600 to 687 units — a nearly 15% increase — to align with the city's population growth.

changes.headline: Lewisboro, NY Town Board schedules July 27 STR discussion; draft proposal circulating
changes.summary: Lewisboro's Town Board has scheduled a July 27, 2026 meeting to resume short-term rental discussions. The board has drafted its own STR proposal but is holding off on codifying it pending public feedback — approximately 40 emails have been received with a slight majority favoring regulation. No ordinance has been adopted.
